mechanism_narrative: Growth in real retail sales (RSAFS) indicates strengthening consumer demand, which supports corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC of RSAFS versus forward 20-day returns is not significantly positive over any 5-year rolling window or the long-short portfolio yields negative Sharpe ratio.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) to above 4.5% signals the onset of a recessionary labor market, reducing consumer spending and corporate profits, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Following UNRATE crossing 4.5%, S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 60% of instances or the average return is not statistically negative (t-test p>0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in industrial production (INDPRO) and rises in unemployment (UNRATE) jointly indicate a broad-based economic contraction, which strongly predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the following 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month change negative for 2 consecutive months and UNRATE increase of at least 0.2 percentage points over the same period
failure_condition: In periods where both conditions are met, the 60-day forward return is positive in more than 50% of cases or the average return is not significantly negative.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle (FEDFUNDS above 3%), rising consumer prices (CPIAUCSL) reinforce expectations of further rate hikes, depressing equity valuations and leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In periods where FEDFUNDS > 3% and CPIAUCSL is rising, the 20-day forward return is positive in more than 60% of instances or the average return is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: A larger-than-expected monthly increase in the CPI (CPIAUCSL month-over-month > 0.3%) surprises markets with inflationary pressure, causing a negative short-term reaction in S&P 500 over the following 5 trading days.
failure_condition: After CPI prints with MoM > 0.3%, the average 5-day S&P 500 return is positive or the proportion of negative returns is not significantly above 50%.

mechanism_narrative: When payroll growth (PAYEMS > 150,000) and housing starts (HOUST > 1,200,000) both signal strong underlying economic momentum, it supports corporate earnings and equity prices, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When both conditions are satisfied, the 60-day forward return is negative in more than 50% of cases or the average return is not significantly positive.

mechanism_narrative: During industrial recession (INDPRO < 100), increases in industrial production indicate a cyclical recovery, which is a positive signal for S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: In periods when INDPRO < 100 and INDPRO rises, the 60-day forward return is negative in more than 50% of cases or the average return is not significantly positive.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in real GDP (GDPC1 quarterly growth annualized < 0%) signals a recession, leading to broad market declines and negative S&P 500 returns over the following 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly growth annualized falls below 0%
failure_condition: Following a quarter of negative GDP growth, the 60-day S&P 500 return is positive in more than 50% of instances or the average return is not significantly negative.
